intitule: Job Application Essentials: Vocabulary and Grammar Check

enonce.texte:
Read the job advertisement below carefully. Then, complete the exercises that follow.


JOB ADVERTISEMENT

Position: Sales Assistant
Location: Douala, Cameroon
Requirements: High school diploma (Première or Terminale). Good communication skills. Ability to work on Saturdays.
Salary: Competitive, based on experience.
How to apply: Send your CV and a cover letter to recruit@bestjobs.cm before May 30th.

questions.texte: Identify three vocabulary words or phrases from the advertisement that relate to job application.
Define the term 'cover letter' as used in the context of a job application.
questions.reponse: A cover letter is a formal document sent with a CV that introduces the applicant, explains their interest in the position, and highlights their relevant qualifications and skills for the job.

questions.texte: Complete the following sentence using the correct modal verb ('should' or 'have to') to express advice or obligation: 'You _______ prepare your CV carefully before sending it to an employer.'
questions.reponse: should (advice) OR have to (obligation). Both are acceptable depending on nuance, but 'should' is the standard answer for advice.

questions.texte: Transform the following sentence from the advertisement into the passive voice: 'You must send your CV and a cover letter to recruit@bestjobs.cm.'
questions.reponse: Your CV and a cover letter must be sent to recruit@bestjobs.cm.

enonce.texte: You are a young graduate looking for a part-time job. Analyze the job advertisement provided in Exercise 1 and answer the following questions to decide if you are a good candidate.

questions.texte: List two requirements from the advertisement that you must meet to apply for this job.
questions.reponse: 1. Have a high school diploma (Première or Terminale). 2. Have good communication skills. 3. Be able to work on Saturdays.

questions.texte: Explain why the deadline (May 30th) is an important element to consider when applying.
questions.reponse: The deadline is crucial because applications sent after May 30th will likely be rejected or not considered. Respecting deadlines shows professionalism and responsibility.

questions.texte: Formulate a polite question using a modal (can, could, may) to ask the employer about the possibility of an interview if you are shortlisted.
questions.reponse: Could you please inform me when interviews will be scheduled if my application is shortlisted?

enonce.texte: You are applying for the Sales Assistant position from the advertisement. Write a short cover letter (120-150 words) addressed to the hiring manager.

questions.texte: Write a cover letter that includes: a polite greeting, an introduction stating the job you are applying for, a paragraph explaining why you are a good fit (mention your communication skills and availability), and a polite closing.
questions.reponse:
**Model Answer:**

Dear Hiring Manager,

I am writing to apply for the Sales Assistant position advertised on your website. I am a Terminale student with a strong interest in developing my professional skills.

I believe I am a good fit for this role because I have excellent communication skills, which I have developed through school presentations and group work. Moreover, I am fully available to work on Saturdays. I am a punctual, motivated, and quick learner.

I have attached my CV for your consideration. I would welcome the opportunity to discuss my application in an interview. Thank you for your time.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]

enonce.texte: Exchange your cover letter from Exercise 3 with a classmate. Use the checklist below to evaluate their letter. Tick (✓) each criterion if it is present and correctly used.

questions.texte: Checklist: 1. Does the letter include a clear subject or reference to the job? 2. Is there a formal greeting (e.g., 'Dear Mr./Ms. ...')? 3. Are the applicant's communication skills mentioned? 4. Is availability on Saturdays stated? 5. Is there a polite closing (e.g., 'Sincerely')? 6. Is the present perfect tense used correctly (e.g., 'I have developed...')?
questions.reponse: Peer feedback will vary. The teacher should ensure students provide constructive comments, e.g., 'Your letter has all 6 points, but check the spelling of 'Sincerely'.'

enonce.texte: Imagine you are in a mock job interview. The interviewer asks you a difficult question. Prepare your answer.

questions.texte: The interviewer asks: 'You have no paid work experience. Why should we hire you?' Justify your answer using the present perfect tense (to talk about your past actions/skills) and a modal of advice (should) to show you are open to learning.
questions.reponse: Example answer: 'That's true, I have not had a paid job before. However, I have participated in several school projects that required teamwork and organization. I have also volunteered at a local event, which helped me develop my communication skills. You should hire me because I am a fast learner, and I am very motivated to prove myself.'
